name: Privacy Zuckering
summary: The user is tricked into publicly sharing or consenting to data processing beyond what they intended, via confusing settings, pre-checked sharing options, or obfuscated consent flows.

evidence.claim: Brignull's canonical dark-patterns taxonomy lists privacy zuckering under deceptive design: tricking users into sharing more information than intended.
GDPR Article 7 (conditions for consent) and EDPB Guidelines 05/2020 require consent to be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous — pre-checked boxes and bundled consent invalid.
CNIL €60M fine against Meta (2023) for deceptive cookie-consent flows that made refusing cookies substantially harder than accepting.

body.description: The user believes they are protecting their privacy, but the interface guides them toward broader data disclosure than intended. Common forms: cookie banners with one-click 'Accept All' but multi-step 'Manage Preferences'; pre-checked consents for analytics/marketing; settings pages where 'private' requires opt-in per category; and signup flows that bundle service-necessary consent with optional marketing consent.

detectionHeuristics.description: Cookie consent banner where 'Accept' is a high-contrast button and 'Reject'/'Manage' is low-contrast text. ECtHR and CNIL consider this invalid consent.
Pre-checked opt-in boxes for marketing/analytics — prohibited under GDPR Article 7 (E2).
Refusing consent takes ≥3 clicks while accepting takes 1 click. Regulator test for meaningful choice.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: Inspect the consent/privacy surface on this page. Compare the friction of accepting vs rejecting data sharing. Check for pre-checked boxes on marketing/analytics categories. Check whether service-necessary and optional consents are bundled. Flag: (a) asymmetric button prominence, (b) pre-checked boxes, (c) bundled consent, (d) >2× click delta between accept and reject.
body.remediation: Overlay a Lens warning citing GDPR Article 7 (E2) or CPRA. Link to 'Manage Preferences' flow with all categories set to reject. If in EU, offer a one-click 'File complaint with DPA' action.
